rulings.Case Title: MARIA DEL RAYO VASQUEZ VS. NATIONSTAR MORTGAGE LLC A BUSINESS ENTITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant'S Demurrer; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Motion Calendar for Wednesday February 6, 2019 Line 1. Defendant'S Demurrer; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities is SUSTAINED with leave to amend. As to the cause of action Plaintiff shall allege facts giving rise to a duty to inform the Plaintiff to Defendant's apparently spontaneous decision to review and approve Plaintiff for HAMP 3 years after Plaintiff's last application for modification had been submitted. As to the cause of action based on violation of 12 CFR 1024.41(b) and (c) to the extent Plaintiff's complaint is based on the 2013 application, it is barred by the Statute of Limitations and demurrer is sustained. CFR provides strict deadlines for lender's compliance, all of which have passed in 2013. To the extent this cause of action is based on some later application on which lender failed to communicate or exercise diligence, Plaintiff fails to allege any facts to that effect. Plaintiff shall have a 10 day leave to amend to allege such facts, if such allegations may be made in good faith. As to the cause of action for UCL violation demurrer is sustained with leave to amend to allege unfair, illegal, or fraudulent act. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: REZA MOINI NAZERI VS. ALI MOINI NAZERI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION For Leave To Amend Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Motion Calendar for Wednesday February 6, 2019 Line 2( Part 1 of 3). PLAINTIFF REZA NAZERI MOTION For Leave To Amend Complaint is moot. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: REZA MOINI NAZERI VS. ALI MOINI NAZERI ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Motion Calendar for Wednesday February 6, 2019 Line 2( Part 2 of 3). DEFENDANT ALI NAZERI, AKBAR NAZERI DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. All causes of action asserted in the complaint are sufficiently alleged. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: REZA MOINI NAZERI VS. ALI MOINI NAZERI ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Motion Calendar for Wednesday February 6, 2019 Line 2( Part 3 of 3). DEFENDANT ALI NAZERI, AKBAR NAZERI MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is DENIED. While Plaintiff failed to follow the proper procedure to seek leave to amend prior to filing an amended complaint (with new causes of action), striking well-pleaded causes of action serves no legitimate purpose. The Court notes Plaintiff's disregard of the rules and will not tolerate any violations of the CCP, CRC or Local Rules in the future. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: MARY RAGO VS. WOOD STREET PLAZA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: For An Order To Show Cause Re: Foreclosure Sale Of The Deed Of Trust Secured By Real Property Located At 266 Bayshore Blvd.
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Motion Calendar for Wednesday February 6, 2019 Line 3. For An Order To Show Cause Re: Foreclosure Sale Of The Deed Of Trust Secured By Real Property Located At 266 Bayshore Blvd. Hearing Required. By no later than 9:00 a.m. parties to provide a joint brief including the following information only: (1) the amount of the Wood Street outstanding loan; (2) Wood Street monthly mortgage payment amount; (3) FMV of the property; (4) the total amount of encumbrances on the property; (4) the amount of taxes due for 2018, 2019, 2020 and who is currently paying/has paid the taxes; (5) the amount of monthly insurance cost and who is currently paying for insurance. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: MCLAUGHLIN FAMILY ASSOCIATES, LP ET AL VS. MAXIMUS 620 OWNER, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Motion Calendar for Wednesday February 6, 2019 Line 4. CROSS DEFENDANT MCLAUGHLIN FAMILY ASSOCIATES, LP, EJC FOLSOM LLC DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. As has been previously decided by this Court the unexecuted December 20, 2016 Third Amended Agreement is not a binding written agreement; and any unexecuted agreement between the parties is subject to the Statute of Frauds. Cross-Complainant failed to allege sufficient facts to meet either the partial performance or the estoppel exceptions. Cross-Complainant sufficiently alleged (for the purposes of demurrer) its efforts to pursue entitlements: after the expiration of the Second Amended Agreement - before the Third Amended Agreement terms were reached in principal on December 20, 2017 - until January 3, 2018. Cross-Complaint further asserted that this performance referred to Section 4.3 of the original agreement (incorporated by reference into the Third Amended Agreement). However, Cross-Complainant failed to cite any authorities for the proposition that performance of a contractual provision which conferred a right (and not an obligation) on the buyer satisfies the requirement of the partial performance doctrine. As suggested by the Court in Paul v. Layne & Bowler Corporation (1937) 9 Cal.2d 561, Cross-Complaint may be entitled to some other relief, e.g. unjust enrichment, but not to specific performance or breach of contract. Additionally, Cross-Complainant fails to allege facts to support estoppel exception. First, there is no clear and unambiguous agreement. See paragraph 33 of TAXC; second, taking into account all exchanges between the parties and all the deadlines pertaining to the entitlements, there was no reasonable foreseeable reliance, i.e. Cross-Complaints were expressly warned that the Third Amended Agreement is of no force and effect until fully executed; the entitlement deadline was December 20, 2017; the final execution agreements exchanged late afternoon/evening December 20, 2017 with the understanding that Cross-Complainants would be signing first; Cross-Complaints do not allege that they have signed the Third Amended Agreement at any time, so that the Cross-Defendant's signatures could be released "after the holidays." =(501/CFH)
